message:
feat: add HackerNews V3 contract planning document, sync Cargo.lock and beads

Add connectors/hackernews/README.md defining the first-slice V3 contract
for fcp.hackernews: read-only Firebase API connector for item lookup,
user lookup, ranked story feeds (top/new/best/ask/show/job), and
health checking. Explicitly defers Algolia search, HTML scraping, and
authenticated YC workflows to follow-on beads.

Also syncs Cargo.lock with workspace changes and updates beads issue
tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1141,15 +1141,15 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","title":"[T1-CLOUD.1] fcp.aws — AWS Multi-Service Connector (Lambda, SQS, SNS, CloudWatch, IAM)","description":"AWS multi-service connector covering the most commonly used AWS services beyond S3
(which we already have). Uses aws-sdk-rust for native Rust AWS SDK integration.

## Services and operations
- **Lambda**: aws.lambda.invoke, aws.lambda.list_functions, aws.lambda.get_function,
  aws.lambda.create_function, aws.lambda.update_code
- **SQS**: aws.sqs.send_message, aws.sqs.receive_messages, aws.sqs.delete_message,
  aws.sqs.list_queues, aws.sqs.get_queue_attributes
- **SNS**: aws.sns.publish, aws.sns.subscribe, aws.sns.list_topics,
  aws.sns.list_subscriptions, aws.sns.create_topic
- **CloudWatch**: aws.cloudwatch.get_metrics, aws.cloudwatch.put_metric,
  aws.cloudwatch.describe_alarms, aws.cloudwatch.get_log_events
- **IAM**: aws.iam.list_users, aws.iam.get_user, aws.iam.list_roles,
  aws.iam.list_policies (read-only for safety)

## Auth model
- AWS credentials via environment (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
- Or secretless credential_id for egress proxy injection
- Region configuration required

## Architecture decision
Single connector crate with per-service modules rather than 5 separate crates.
This mirrors how aws-sdk-rust is structured and reduces manifest proliferation.
Each service is a module (lambda.rs, sqs.rs, sns.rs, cloudwatch.rs, iam.rs)
with its own operation set, but they share the AWS credential + region config.

## Network constraints
- host_allow: [*.amazonaws.com, *.aws.amazon.com]
- Region-specific endpoints

## Safety tiers
- Lambda invoke, SQS send/receive, SNS publish: Risky (side effects)
- IAM operations: Safe (read-only)
- Lambda create/update, SNS create_topic: Dangerous (infrastructure mutation)

## 4 subtasks: types/client/error, connector/manifest, per-service modules, tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:31.436261Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02.148874Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:31.436261Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1787,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements: 50+ unit tests (wiremock), 10+ integration tests (ConnectorSuite/ComplianceSuite), E2E compliance script, structured JSON logging with connector_id/operation_id/duration_ms, retry telemetry with attempt/delay_ms/error. All tests deterministic, no real API calls, no timing-based flakiness.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","title":"AWS: V3 contract, scope, auth model, and service inventory","description":"Define the V3 contract for AWS. Pin down the exact first-slice service inventory across Lambda, SQS, SNS, CloudWatch, and IAM, the auth boundary around IAM credentials and role assumption, OperationInfo safety and risk semantics, and explicit non-goals so the multi-service connector stays coherent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:22.599619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:39:39.805293Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:39:39.804876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:22.599619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:22.599619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:22.599619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1842,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for AWS:

**Archetype**: Cloud Control Plane (multi-service)
**Auth model**: AWS IAM credentials (access_key_id + secret_access_key), IAM role (instance profile), or STS assume-role. Region required. credential_id for secretless.
**Network**: *.amazonaws.com:443, *.aws.amazon.com:443. TLS required.

**Operations (first slice — S3/EC2/Lambda/STS focus)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| aws.s3.list_buckets | Safe | Low | None | aws.s3.read |
| aws.s3.list_objects | Safe | Low | None | aws.s3.read |
| aws.s3.get_object | Safe | Low | None | aws.s3.read |
| aws.s3.put_object | Risky | Medium | Strict | aws.s3.write |
| aws.s3.delete_object | Dangerous | High | Strict | aws.s3.write |
| aws.ec2.describe_instances | Safe | Low | None | aws.ec2.read |
| aws.ec2.start_instance | Risky | Medium | Strict | aws.ec2.write |
| aws.ec2.stop_instance | Risky | Medium | Strict | aws.ec2.write |
| aws.ec2.terminate_instance | Dangerous | Critical | Strict | aws.ec2.write |
| aws.lambda.list_functions | Safe | Low | None | aws.lambda.read |
| aws.lambda.invoke | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | aws.lambda.write |
| aws.sts.get_caller_identity | Safe | Low | None | aws.iam.read |
| aws.health | Safe | Low | Strict | aws.iam.read |

**Safety policy**: Terminate instance = Dangerous/Critical. Delete operations across all services = Dangerous. IAM/permission operations deferred to Critical tier in future slice. Region scoping explicit in every operation input.

**Provisioning**: AWS credentials via environment, config file, or IAM role. Doctor: STS GetCallerIdentity validates credentials.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No IAM management, no CloudFormation/CDK, no RDS/DynamoDB (separate connectors if needed), no billing/cost explorer, no VPC management.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:39:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","title":"AWS: typed client foundation, service modules, runtime, and manifest","description":"Build the AWS connector implementation: typed config and auth, per-service client modules, pagination and async job handling, ConnectorRuntime lifecycle, truthful manifest declarations, capability scope, network constraints, and typed introspection across the chosen AWS surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryAnchor","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-20T00:50:07Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T00:50:07Z","close_reason":"Verified existing AWS connector implementation and normalized formatting; rch cargo test -p fcp-aws and rch cargo clippy -p fcp-aws --all-targets -- -D warnings passed, and rustfmt --check is clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:37.023222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1866,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"VERIFIED by SunnyMoose: AWS connector fully implemented. 74 lib tests passing.","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:47:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","title":"AWS: readiness, compliance, verification, and operator guidance","description":"Finish AWS with readiness and doctor outputs, credential and permission remediation guidance, deterministic tests across each included AWS service slice, and explicit V3 compliance evidence that proves the connector is truthful about capabilities and failure modes.","acceptance_criteria":"- closeout requires comprehensive deterministic unit coverage for auth, timeout, retry, error-mapping, pagination or state transitions, and the highest-risk provider-specific mutations
- closeout requires archetype-appropriate integration coverage built on shared `fcp-testkit` helpers rather than connector-local one-offs
- closeout requires at least one replayable E2E or host-backed verification script that runs through `rch`, captures detailed structured logs, and preserves manifest, readiness, doctor, and conformance artifacts
- operator guidance must name prerequisites, dedicated environments, redaction rules, common remediation paths, and rerun commands for the verification bundle
- evidence attached to the bead must be sufficient for a future agent to rerun verification without reopening external planning notes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:28:29.036588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:45.067794Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","title":"AWS: readiness, compliance, verification, and operator guidance","description":"Finish AWS with readiness and doctor outputs, credential and permission remediation guidance, deterministic tests across each included AWS service slice, and explicit V3 compliance evidence that proves the connector is truthful about capabilities and failure modes.","acceptance_criteria":"- closeout requires comprehensive deterministic unit coverage for auth, timeout, retry, error-mapping, pagination or state transitions, and the highest-risk provider-specific mutations

- evidence attached to the bead must be sufficient for a future agent to rerun verification without reopening external planning notes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:32:46Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T17:32:46Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:18:45.067794Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:02:51.438704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","title":"[T1-CLOUD.2] fcp.gcp — Google Cloud Platform Connector (Compute, Run, Pub/Sub, GKE)","description":"GCP multi-service connector for core Google Cloud services. We have Google Workspace APIs (Sheets, Docs, Calendar, etc.) but not the cloud infrastructure APIs. Operations: gcp.compute.list_instances, gcp.compute.start/stop_instance; gcp.run.list_services, gcp.run.deploy_service, gcp.run.get_service; gcp.pubsub.publish, gcp.pubsub.pull, gcp.pubsub.list_topics, gcp.pubsub.list_subscriptions; gcp.gke.list_clusters, gcp.gke.get_cluster. Auth: service account JSON key or workload identity. Network: *.googleapis.com. Uses google-cloud-rust crate. Single crate with per-service modules. 4 subtasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:44.106138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02.472243Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:44.106138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1788,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements: 50+ unit tests (wiremock), 10+ integration tests (ConnectorSuite/ComplianceSuite), E2E compliance script, structured JSON logging with connector_id/operation_id/duration_ms, retry telemetry with attempt/delay_ms/error. All tests deterministic, no real API calls, no timing-based flakiness.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","title":"GCP: V3 contract, scope, auth model, and service inventory","description":"Define the V3 contract for GCP. Pin down the exact first-slice service inventory across Compute, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, and GKE, the auth boundary around service accounts and project scoping, OperationInfo safety and risk semantics, and explicit non-goals for the first implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:05.799576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:40:52.441483Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:40:44.500287Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:05.799576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:05.799576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:05.799576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1843,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for GCP:

**Archetype**: Cloud Control Plane (multi-service, complements existing Google Workspace connectors)
**Auth model**: Service account JSON key, Application Default Credentials, or OAuth 2.0. Project ID required. Reuses fcp-google-discovery substrate for auth.
**Network**: *.googleapis.com:443. TLS required. Google Workspace network template.

**Operations (first slice — Compute/Cloud Storage/Cloud Run/IAM focus)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| gcp.compute.list_instances | Safe | Low | None | gcp.compute.read |
| gcp.compute.get_instance | Safe | Low | None | gcp.compute.read |
| gcp.compute.start_instance | Risky | Medium | Strict | gcp.compute.write |
| gcp.compute.stop_instance | Risky | Medium | Strict | gcp.compute.write |
| gcp.compute.delete_instance | Dangerous | Critical | Strict | gcp.compute.write |
| gcp.storage.list_objects | Safe | Low | None | gcp.storage.read |
| gcp.storage.get_object | Safe | Low | None | gcp.storage.read |
| gcp.storage.upload_object | Risky | Medium | Strict | gcp.storage.write |
| gcp.storage.delete_object | Dangerous | High | Strict | gcp.storage.write |
| gcp.run.list_services | Safe | Low | None | gcp.run.read |
| gcp.run.deploy_service | Risky | High | Strict | gcp.run.write |
| gcp.run.delete_service | Dangerous | Critical | Strict | gcp.run.write |
| gcp.projects.get | Safe | Low | None | gcp.iam.read |
| gcp.health | Safe | Low | Strict | gcp.iam.read |

**Safety policy**: Delete instance/service = Dangerous/Critical. Deploy = Risky/High (cost implications, traffic shift). IAM policy modifications deferred to Critical tier. Project/region scoping explicit.

**Provisioning**: Service account or ADC. Doctor: projects.get validates credentials and project access.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No IAM policy management, no GKE, no Cloud SQL (use PostgreSQL/MySQL connectors), no Pub/Sub (separate connector), no BigQuery (existing connector).","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:40:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","title":"GCP: typed client foundation, service modules, runtime, and manifest","description":"Build the GCP connector implementation: typed config and auth, per-service client modules, long-running operation handling, paging, ConnectorRuntime lifecycle, truthful manifest declarations, capability scope, network constraints, and typed introspection across the chosen GCP surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryAnchor","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-20T00:51:27Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T00:51:27Z","close_reason":"Verified existing GCP connector implementation and normalized formatting; rch cargo test -p fcp-gcp and rch cargo clippy -p fcp-gcp --all-targets -- -D warnings passed, and rustfmt --check is clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:20.094226Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1867,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"VERIFIED by SunnyMoose: GCP connector fully implemented. 74 lib tests passing.","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:47:29Z"}]}

- evidence attached to the bead must be sufficient for a future agent to rerun verification without reopening external planning notes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:28:29.064107Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:19:01.009265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","title":"GCP: readiness, compliance, verification, and operator guidance","description":"Finish GCP with readiness and doctor outputs, project and IAM remediation guidance, deterministic tests across each included GCP service slice, and explicit V3 compliance evidence that proves the connector behaves predictably under auth, quota, and API failure conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"- closeout requires comprehensive deterministic unit coverage for auth, timeout, retry, error-mapping, pagination or state transitions, and the highest-risk provider-specific mutations
- closeout requires archetype-appropriate integration coverage built on shared `fcp-testkit` helpers rather than connector-local one-offs
- closeout requires at least one replayable E2E or host-backed verification script that runs through `rch`, captures detailed structured logs, and preserves manifest, readiness, doctor, and conformance artifacts
- operator guidance must name prerequisites, dedicated environments, redaction rules, common remediation paths, and rerun commands for the verification bundle
- evidence attached to the bead must be sufficient for a future agent to rerun verification without reopening external planning notes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-21T18:11:50.192307Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T18:11:50.191507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:19:01.009265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:34.492028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3","title":"[T1-CLOUD.3] fcp.azure — Azure Cloud Connector (VMs, Functions, Storage, Service Bus)","description":"Azure cloud connector for core Microsoft Azure services. Partial coverage exists via Microsoft365 connector but dedicated Azure infra connector needed. Operations: azure.vm.list, azure.vm.start/stop/restart; azure.functions.invoke, azure.functions.list; azure.storage.list_blobs, azure.storage.upload/download_blob; azure.servicebus.send_message, azure.servicebus.receive_messages; azure.resource_groups.list. Auth: Azure AD OAuth2 with service principal (client_id/client_secret/tenant_id). Network: *.azure.com, *.core.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com. REST APIs throughout. 4 subtasks.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:56.649857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02.797686Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:56:56.649857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1789,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements: 50+ unit tests (wiremock), 10+ integration tests (ConnectorSuite/ComplianceSuite), E2E compliance script, structured JSON logging with connector_id/operation_id/duration_ms, retry telemetry with attempt/delay_ms/error. All tests deterministic, no real API calls, no timing-based flakiness.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3.1","title":"Azure: V3 contract, scope, auth model, and service inventory","description":"Define the V3 contract for Azure. Pin down the exact first-slice service inventory across VMs, Functions, Storage, and Service Bus, the auth boundary around Azure AD and tenant or subscription scoping, OperationInfo safety and risk semantics, and explicit non-goals for the first implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:48.943500Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:43:18.044764Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:43:10.382396Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:48.943500Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:48.943500Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:03:48.943500Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1844,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.2.3.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3 contract for Azure:

**Archetype**: Cloud Control Plane (multi-service)
**Auth model**: Service principal (client_id + client_secret + tenant_id) or Managed Identity. Subscription ID required. OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow to login.microsoftonline.com.
**Network**: *.azure.com:443, management.azure.com:443, login.microsoftonline.com:443. TLS required.

**Operations (first slice — VM/Storage/App Service focus)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| azure.vm.list | Safe | Low | None | azure.compute.read |
| azure.vm.get | Safe | Low | None | azure.compute.read |
| azure.vm.start | Risky | Medium | Strict | azure.compute.write |
| azure.vm.stop | Risky | Medium | Strict | azure.compute.write |
| azure.vm.delete | Dangerous | Critical | Strict | azure.compute.write |
| azure.storage.list_containers | Safe | Low | None | azure.storage.read |
| azure.storage.upload_blob | Risky | Medium | Strict | azure.storage.write |
| azure.storage.download_blob | Safe | Low | None | azure.storage.read |
| azure.storage.delete_blob | Dangerous | High | Strict | azure.storage.write |
| azure.appservice.list_apps | Safe | Low | None | azure.app.read |
| azure.appservice.deploy | Risky | High | Strict | azure.app.write |
| azure.subscription.get | Safe | Low | None | azure.iam.read |
| azure.health | Safe | Low | Strict | azure.iam.read |

**Safety policy**: VM delete = Dangerous/Critical. Blob delete = Dangerous/High. Deploy = Risky/High. RBAC management deferred. Subscription/resource group scoping explicit in every operation.

**Provisioning**: Service principal creation in Azure AD. Doctor: subscription.get validates auth.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No RBAC/IAM management, no AKS, no Azure SQL (use database connectors), no Azure DevOps, no Cosmos DB, no Azure Functions management.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:42:46Z"}]}

**Archetype**: Cloud Control Plane (edge infrastructure)
**Auth model**: API Token (scoped) or Global API Key + Email. API Token recommended (least-privilege). Bearer token auth.
**Network**: api.cloudflare.com:443. TLS required.

**Operations (first slice — Workers/Pages/DNS/KV focus)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| cloudflare.workers.list | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.workers.read |
| cloudflare.workers.get | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.workers.read |
| cloudflare.workers.deploy | Risky | High | Strict | cloudflare.workers.write |
| cloudflare.workers.delete | Dangerous | Critical | Strict | cloudflare.workers.write |
| cloudflare.pages.list_projects | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.pages.read |
| cloudflare.pages.create_deployment | Risky | High | Strict | cloudflare.pages.write |
| cloudflare.dns.list_records | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.dns.read |
| cloudflare.dns.create_record | Risky | Medium | Strict | cloudflare.dns.write |
| cloudflare.dns.update_record | Risky | Medium | Strict | cloudflare.dns.write |
| cloudflare.dns.delete_record | Dangerous | High | Strict | cloudflare.dns.write |
| cloudflare.kv.get | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.kv.read |
| cloudflare.kv.put | Risky | Medium | Strict | cloudflare.kv.write |
| cloudflare.kv.delete | Dangerous | High | Strict | cloudflare.kv.write |
| cloudflare.zones.list | Safe | Low | None | cloudflare.zones.read |
| cloudflare.health | Safe | Low | Strict | cloudflare.zones.read |

**Safety policy**: Worker/DNS delete = Dangerous (affects live traffic). Deploy = Risky/High (replaces running code). Zone ID scoping required for zone-specific operations.

**Provisioning**: Create API token at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. Doctor: zones.list validates token.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No R2 storage management, no D1 database, no Queues, no WAF/firewall rules, no Access/Zero Trust, no registrar management.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:44:02Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,143 @@
+# Hacker News Connector V3 Contract
+
+> **Status**: planning contract
+> **Bead**: `flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.2.1`
+> **Unblocks**: `flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.2.2`
+> **Primary upstream**: Hacker News Firebase API at `https://github.com/HackerNews/API`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This document fixes the first implementation slice for `fcp.hackernews` so the follow-on runtime bead can converge on a stable contract rather than conflating the public Firebase API with other Hacker News data sources such as Algolia search, HTML scraping, or authenticated YC workflows.
+
+The connector is a public, read-only Hacker News Firebase API client for item lookup, user lookup, ranked story feeds, and health checking.
+
+## Current Runtime Snapshot
+
+The current crate already exposes these operations:
+
+- `hackernews.item.get`
+- `hackernews.user.get`
+- `hackernews.top_stories`
+- `hackernews.new_stories`
+- `hackernews.best_stories`
+- `hackernews.ask_stories`
+- `hackernews.show_stories`
+- `hackernews.job_stories`
+- `hackernews.health`
+
+Important runtime truths that the contract must preserve:
+
+- Authentication is optional because the Firebase API surface used here is public.
+- Configuration is only `base_url`, retry policy, and `request_timeout_ms`.
+- The default API root is `https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0`.
+- `base_url` is overrideable, which is useful for tests or mirrors, but the production contract is the Firebase host.
+- `item.get` returns raw Hacker News items by numeric ID and covers stories, comments, jobs, polls, and poll options.
+- `item.get` treats Firebase `null` responses as not found.
+- Feed operations return numeric IDs only. They do not expand stories into full item payloads.
+- `top_stories`, `new_stories`, and `best_stories` can return up to 500 IDs from the provider; `ask_stories`, `show_stories`, and `job_stories` can return up to 200.
+- The optional `limit` parameter is applied locally after the feed response is fetched.
+- `health` uses the top-stories endpoint as a reachability probe.
+- The connector exposes no streaming, replay, or write surface.
+
+## First-Slice Scope
+
+The first Hacker News slice is intentionally narrow:
+
+- Read a single item by numeric ID.
+- Read a single user profile by username.
+- Read ranked story feed IDs from the public Firebase API.
+- Read category-specific Ask HN, Show HN, and Jobs feed IDs.
+- Verify API reachability and rate-limit behavior through a health probe.
+
+This slice is intentionally closer to "typed public API mirror" than to "full Hacker News product surface."
+
+## Service Inventory
+
+| Surface | Status in first slice | Notes |
+|---------|-----------------------|-------|
+| Item lookup | In scope | Reads a single story, comment, job, poll, or poll option by numeric ID. |
+| User lookup | In scope | Reads a single public user profile by username. |
+| Ranked feeds | In scope | Top, new, best, Ask, Show, and Jobs feed ID lists are implemented. |
+| Comments | Partial | Comments are reachable through `item.get`, but there is no recursive thread expansion or tree flattening operation. |
+| Search | Out of scope | No Algolia-backed search or query surface exists in the current connector. |
+| Writes | Out of scope | No submit, vote, favorite, login, reply, or moderation operations exist. |
+| Streaming | Out of scope | No event feed, websocket, polling subscription, or replay surface exists. |
+
+## Auth And Scope Boundary
+
+- There is no external auth flow in the first slice.
+- The connector's trust boundary is the configured base URL, the public-zone policy, and the single `hackernews.read` capability.
+- All exposed operations require only `hackernews.read`.
+- Usernames are sanitized before being inserted into the request path and must not contain `/`, `\\`, `..`, or null bytes.
+- Numeric item IDs are the stable primary identifiers for item retrieval.
+- The manifest is public/read-only oriented: home zone is `z:public`, allowed sources are `z:public` and `z:work`, allowed targets are `z:public`, required capabilities are `network.dns` and `network.outbound`, and forbidden capabilities include `system.exec` and `system.privileged`.
+
+## Network And Runtime Invariants
+
+- Production host: `hacker-news.firebaseio.com`
+- Default path prefix: `/v0`
+- Port: `443`
+- TLS + SNI required
+- `deny_localhost = true`
+- `deny_private_ranges = true`
+- `deny_tailnet_ranges = true`
+- `deny_ip_literals = true`
+- `max_redirects = 2`
+- Default request timeout is `30_000 ms`
+- Runtime uses retry-aware GET logic for normal data fetches, including `429` handling with `Retry-After`
+- `health` is a lighter direct GET probe against `topstories.json`
+
+## Capability Families
+
+| Capability | Purpose |
+|-----------|---------|
+| `hackernews.read` | Read public items, users, feed ID lists, and API health |
+
+## Operation Inventory
+
+| Operation | Provider endpoint target | Capability | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | Idempotency | Rationale |
+|-----------|--------------------------|------------|------------|-----------|-------------|-----------|
+| `hackernews.item.get` | `GET /item/{id}.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `Strict` | Deterministic point read of a single public item ID. |
+| `hackernews.user.get` | `GET /user/{username}.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `Strict` | Deterministic point read of one public user profile. |
+| `hackernews.top_stories` | `GET /topstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only ranked feed snapshot returning story IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.new_stories` | `GET /newstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only newest-stories snapshot returning IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.best_stories` | `GET /beststories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only best-stories snapshot returning IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.ask_stories` | `GET /askstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only Ask HN feed snapshot returning IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.show_stories` | `GET /showstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only Show HN feed snapshot returning IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.job_stories` | `GET /jobstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `None` | Read-only Jobs feed snapshot returning IDs only. |
+| `hackernews.health` | `GET /topstories.json` | `hackernews.read` | `Safe` | `Low` | `Strict` | Reachability and rate-limit probe for the public Firebase API. |
+
+## Explicit Non-Goals
+
+The first Hacker News slice does not include these surfaces:
+
+- Algolia search or relevance-ranked query APIs
+- authenticated user actions such as submit, vote, favorite, reply, or login
+- HTML scraping of `news.ycombinator.com`
+- recursive comment-tree expansion or denormalized thread rendering helpers
+- item batching, feed enrichment, or automatic expansion from IDs into full story payloads
+- moderation, admin, or YC-internal workflows
+- streaming subscriptions, polling cursors, or live-update delivery
+
+These are excluded on purpose:
+
+- The current connector does not implement them.
+- The public Firebase API is small and stable, which makes it a good first truthful slice.
+- Mixing Firebase reads with Algolia or scraped HTML would create a muddled contract with inconsistent semantics and failure modes.
+
+## Implementation Notes For `flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.2.2`
+
+- Keep the first slice anchored to the Firebase API unless there is an explicit contract revision that adds Algolia as a separate surface.
+- Preserve the current distinction between point reads and feed snapshots: feed operations return IDs only, and `item.get` is the expansion primitive.
+- Keep username path sanitization mechanical and conservative.
+- Preserve retry-aware handling for ordinary fetches and explicit `429 Retry-After` treatment.
+- Revisit the current idempotency split intentionally if needed, but keep manifest and runtime aligned if semantics change.
+- Tests should cover base URL override behavior, `null` item handling, username sanitization, rate-limit classification, feed limiting, and self-check degradation for retryable failures.
+
+## Source Notes
+
+This contract is grounded in the current connector implementation and manifest:
+
+- `connectors/hackernews/src/client.rs` defines the Firebase endpoint paths, retry behavior, username sanitization, `null` item handling, and health probe behavior.
+- `connectors/hackernews/src/connector.rs` defines the public config surface, the single read-capability boundary, and the runtime `OperationInfo` metadata.
+- `connectors/hackernews/manifest.toml` defines the public-zone network policy and read-only connector posture.
